results.title: India Launches Smart Warehousing System to Modernise Foodgrain Storage
results.description: The Smart Warehousing System integrates cutting-edge technologies such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), the Internet of Things (IoT), automation, and advanced analytics. These features will help streamline warehouse operations, improve governance, and enhance visibility across storage facilities. The post India Launches Smart Warehousing System to Modernise Foodgrain Storage appeared first on Dharmakshethra - India Unabridged .

results.title: SubscriberWrites: Can India Become the World’s AI Back Office?
results.description: The idea looks promising. In the race to implement AI systems, companies require huge amounts of human labor behind the scenes. AI models require data that has been labeled, regulated and validated.

results.title: Someone turned this Anywheel bike into a mini cargo bike — and locked it up
results.description: A Tampines resident was surprised to find what appeared to be an Anywheel shared bicycle modified with a wagon trailer and locked together with a personal bicycle at a void deck.

results.title: Dream project of bullet train will be operational in 2027: Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw
results.description: Union Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw on Sunday said that India’s dream bullet train project will become operational in 2027 and will be opened section by section. Speaking on the sidelines of the 12th annual International Day of Yoga in Bikaner, Vaishnaw said, “The dream project of the bullet train will be operational in 2027 and will open section by section. This dream will be fulfilled in 2027.” The Mumbai–Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail Corridor, India’s first bullet train project, is currently under construction. While work on the corridor has progressed in phases, the government has reiterated its commitment to completing and operationalising the project in 2027. India’s high-speed rail ambitions, however, extend far beyond the Mumbai–Ahmedabad route. As part of its long-term vision to build a nationwide bullet train network, the government has already identified seven additional high-speed rail corridors. #WATCH | Bikaner | Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw says, "The dream project of the bullet train will be operational in 2027 and will open section by section. This dream will be fulfilled in 2027" pic.twitter.com/wFAy0ydIpz — ANI (@ANI) June 21, 2026 The proposed routes include Mumbai -Pune, Pune-Hyderabad, Hyderabad- Bengaluru , Hyderabad-Chennai, Chennai-Bengaluru, Delhi -Varanasi and Varanasi-Siliguri. The expansion plans underline the government's broader strategy of developing a modern, high-speed rail network across India, with the Mumbai-Ahmedabad Bullet Train project serving as the foundation for future corridors.
